data.title: Exorcise the haunted office printer
data.description:
## Context
Every night at 03:00 the third-floor LaserJet wakes up and prints one page of
EBCDIC that loosely translates to "FEED ME TONER". Facilities has tried unplugging
it; it printed an apology and turned itself back on.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Printer no longer prints unbidden between 00:00 and 06:00.
- [ ] No paranormal activity for 7 consecutive nights.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause identified and documented.
- [ ] All child tickets closed.

data.title: Stop the 3am EBCDIC séance
data.description:
## What
Intercept the cron-from-beyond that triggers the nightly print job.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identify the trigger (suspected: a possessed at(1) job).
- [ ] Disable it without angering the printer.

src/printer/scheduler.py

data.body: Found it. There is an `at` job owned by uid 666 that pipes /dev/spooky to lp. The job has no crontab entry — it re-creates itself on delete. Escalating: this is less 'bug' and more 'haunting'.

data.title: Fax line emits a low moan after each exorcism attempt
data.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Delete the uid-666 at(1) job.
2. Wait ~30s.

**Expected:** silence.
**Actual:** the fax modem plays a single mournful tone, then the at job returns.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sever the link between the fax modem and the resurrecting job.

data.body: Confirmed independently — my clone replayed grace's events after a sync and converged to the same state with no conflict. The union merge Just Works, even for hauntings.

data.signature: <hmac-over-ticket-id+manifest, computed with the environment signing key>
